characters.physical_details: Lean painter with hazel eyes, paint-callused hands, and an X-Acto scar on his right index finger.

key_relationships.dani_solis: coworker, close friend, near-lover complicated by his secret sabotage
key_relationships.tommy_reyes: coworker and friend who confides his AI-art competition scam
key_relationships.bao_nguyen: coworker and mentor figure whose firing becomes Nick's sharpest guilt point
key_relationships.priya_acharya: manager who promotes and trusts him
key_relationships.marcus_webb: VP who selects him for calibration and directly questions him later

characters.occupation_status: Painter / children's-book illustrator at start; Canopy evaluator from Feb. 2023 at $20/hour; senior evaluator from Mar. 2023 at $22/hour; calibration-team analyst from Sept. 18, 2023 at $60K salary.
characters.literacy_languages: English (native); functional Spanish; trained painter; self-taught basic Python and JavaScript; no formal CS training.

state_transitions.change: Magpie drops him for Midjourney; he loses his last reliable client, faces a rent crisis, and forms the sabotage plan.
Hired by Canopy after passing the visual-analysis interview despite weak coding.
Promoted to senior evaluator because his anatomy and lighting scores lead the floor.
Discovers the calibration-anchor exploit and confirms small senior-level misratings will not flag.
Accepts Marcus's calibration-team offer after Dani is passed over.
Transfers to calibration (effective Sept. 1; pod start Sept. 18) and begins anchor corruption in early October.
Finds seven of his own Vera Lux paintings in a calibration batch and anchors them himself.
Attempts to paint again, fails, and pulls away from Dani after they kiss.
Completes the three-week flagship sabotage and becomes physically ill afterward.
Learns the sabotage has become the viral 'Digital Grotesque' / Soft Error aesthetic.
Fails repeated chances to confess, takes over Q3-Q4 anchor cleanup, and returns to portal rating instead of painting.

characters.physical_details: Dark-haired evaluator in jeans and work boots, often with paint residue under her nails.

key_relationships.nick_bray: coworker and growing romantic connection damaged by his secrecy
key_relationships.luna_solis: daughter and core stake in her custody case
key_relationships.tommy_reyes: coworker she treats with dry affection
key_relationships.priya_acharya: manager who values her but cannot protect her

characters.occupation_status: Former muralist; Canopy evaluator since Oct. 2022; passed over for calibration in Aug. 2023; restructuring threatens her custody case in Mar. 2024.
characters.literacy_languages: English and Spanish; visually literate from mural work; no coding background.

state_transitions.change: Her need to document 'stable employment' for custody becomes explicit.
Passed over for the calibration-team opening Nick receives.
Kisses Nick, then is left without an explanation when he pulls away.
Receives restructuring notice; even a brief payroll gap could be used against her in court.
Takes Bao's handwritten notes and avoids an immediate senior-floor cut when Priya secures Nick's recovery coverage.

characters.physical_details: Thin evaluator with a buzz cut, thrift-store clothes, and chronic dark circles.

key_relationships.nick_bray: coworker and confidant on his AI-art competition scam
key_relationships.dani_solis: coworker he likes and defers to
key_relationships.priya_acharya: manager who keeps trying to correct his workflow

characters.occupation_status: RISD dropout after three semesters; Canopy evaluator at about $21/hour; secretly enters AI work in competitions under a pseudonym; placed on a 60-day performance plan in Mar. 2024.
characters.literacy_languages: English (native); digitally fluent; proficient with Photoshop and AI-image tools.

state_transitions.change: Reveals his AI-art competition scheme and winning prizes, including the $500 Ortega prize.
Rates one of Nick's paintings in the training batch without knowing it is Nick's work.
Receives a probation / performance-improvement letter tied to audit dragnet metrics.

characters.physical_details: Compact older man with silver hair, reading glasses on a chain, neat shirts, and careful designer's hands.

key_relationships.nick_bray: coworker and genuine friend bonded through color, light, and visual language
key_relationships.mai_nguyen: wife; her care needs determine his work life
key_relationships.priya_acharya: manager who admires his quality but cannot shield him from throughput metrics

characters.occupation_status: Retired graphic designer with 41 years in print / packaging; Canopy evaluator covering Mai's caregiver gap; repeatedly warned for slow throughput despite elite quality; fired in Mar. 2024.
characters.literacy_languages: English and Vietnamese; expert in typography, color, print, and composition.

state_transitions.change: Explains Mai's illness, Maria's caregiver schedule, and the speed warnings he keeps receiving.
Recognizes one of Nick's paintings as likely human reference material and recommends reclassification.
Fired for throughput deviation during the San Leandro audit and leaves his handwritten evaluation notes behind.

characters.physical_details: Former teacher in business-casual clothes, early gray at the temples, dark circles, and a Costco antacid bottle nearby.

key_relationships.dani_solis: valued evaluator she cannot protect from corporate decisions
key_relationships.bao_nguyen: high-quality evaluator she repeatedly tries to defend
key_relationships.marcus_webb: VP whose quality mission she shares but whose power she cannot match

characters.occupation_status: Former high school art teacher; San Leandro floor lead, managing evaluators for about 11 months by Mar. 2023.
characters.literacy_languages: English (native), Hindi (conversational), art-education background, corporate data literacy.

state_transitions.change: Promotes Nick to senior evaluator.
Throughput escalation and retraining burden start showing physically through antacids, lack of sleep, and constant triage.
Has a panic attack in the women's bathroom during the audit aftermath.
Tells Nick she is thinking about leaving Canopy.

characters.physical_details: Tall graying tech executive in good sneakers and untucked oxfords, with a leather notebook and an expensive watch he touches when stressed.

key_relationships.nick_bray: calibration recruit he trusts and later questions directly
key_relationships.priya_acharya: floor lead and ally in quality work
key_relationships.dani_solis: strong evaluator he considers but ultimately passes over for calibration

characters.occupation_status: VP of Quality at Canopy; former Google executive; believer in the human layer; leads calibration expansion and recovery; stock options vest in 2025.
characters.literacy_languages: English; technically fluent in AI, QA, and model-training concepts.

state_transitions.change: Visits San Leandro, offers Nick calibration-team role, and formalizes Sept. 2023 transfer.
Leads Manila / residual-drift audit and imposes weekly spot checks on anchors.
Questions Nick about Q3-Q4 anatomy / lighting bias, assigns him anchor-cluster cleanup, and announces the 500-evaluator recovery expansion.

characters.availability: In person in chapters 10, 14, and 18; on email / Slack elsewhere.

characters.physical_details: Small dark-haired second-grader who constantly draws horses.

key_relationships.nick_bray: mother's coworker she likes and invites to her recital

characters.literacy_languages: English and Spanish developing; prolific drawer.

state_transitions.change: Performs in the holiday recital and shows Nick her horse drawing 'Fire.'

characters.availability: Physically appears in chapter 13; referenced elsewhere.

characters.physical_details: Off-page except by text and phone.

key_relationships.nick_bray: old art-school friend still making work honestly

characters.occupation_status: Working painter; at a Taos-area residency while still paying rent in Portland.

timeline.story_start: Late November 2022 in Fruitvale, Oakland, just after Magpie drops Nick for Midjourney.
timeline.estimated_span: About 17 months, from late Nov. 2022 to April 2024.

key_events.event: Magpie drops Nick for Midjourney; rent crisis and sabotage plan begin.
Nick self-teaches Python and LeetCode; applies to Canopy on Jan. 27.
Nick interviews, is offered the job, and starts Canopy.
Nick's evaluation skill becomes visible; Priya promotes him to senior evaluator.
Night shifts reveal the weighted pipeline, Bao's home situation, and throughput fragility.
CEO raises target to 14.2 images per hour; San Leandro expands from 31 to 53 evaluators; Tommy reveals his AI-art competition scam.
Nick identifies the calibration-anchor exploit and tests low-level misratings.
Rosie's outing deepens the team bond and Nick's attraction to Dani.
Marcus offers Nick the calibration role; transfer effective Sept. 1, pod start Sept. 18.
Nick joins calibration, starts anchor corruption in early Oct., and sees Vera Lux gallery material inside the pipeline.
Nick's own Vera Lux paintings appear in a calibration batch and are scored by coworkers.
Nick's attempt to paint fails; Luna's recital and the kiss / withdrawal with Dani follow.
Audit blames Manila throughput gaming, fires 17 Manila evaluators, and leaves residual unexplained drift.
Nick executes the concentrated three-week sabotage across the flagship calibration pipeline.
The corrupted outputs go viral as 'Digital Grotesque' and are monetized by Soft Error.
San Leandro audit fires Bao, puts Tommy on a performance plan, threatens Dani's payroll continuity, and breaks Priya's composure.
Nick fails to confess, inherits Q3-Q4 anchor cleanup, and Canopy announces 500 new evaluators across Austin, Raleigh, and San Leandro.

geography.primary_setting: Oakland and San Leandro in the East Bay, with Canopy's San Francisco headquarters present through screens.

key_locations.name: Nick's Fruitvale studio apartment
key_locations.details: About 500 square feet with east windows, a kitchenette, stacked canvases, and the BART track outside.

key_locations.name: Canopy AI evaluation floor
key_locations.details: Suite 200 in a beige Davis Street business-park building; fluorescent floor with dual-monitor stations, break room, stairwell, and conference rooms.

key_locations.name: Canopy calibration room / fishbowl
key_locations.details: Card-reader room at the end of the second-floor hall; six workstations, larger monitors, quieter HVAC, local anchor team.

key_locations.name: Rosie's bar
key_locations.details: Dive bar a block from the business park; sticky tables, torn pool felt, dead neon O, jukebox, and cheap Tecates.

key_locations.name: Dani's Temescal apartment
key_locations.details: Two-bedroom Temescal fourplex with Luna's horse drawings across the fridge and the kitchen where Nick and Dani kiss.

key_locations.name: Luna's school gym
key_locations.details: Oakland elementary-school gym set up with folding chairs for the holiday recital.

key_locations.name: Vera Lux
key_locations.details: Small Temescal gallery that hosted Nick's last show and kept the unsold paintings online.

key_locations.name: Canopy San Francisco HQ
key_locations.details: Executive office used for all-hands, Marcus's base, and later recovery planning.

distances: Fruitvale to the San Leandro office is about 15-20 minutes by car, longer by BART plus walking.
Rosie's is across the parking lot and about a block from the Canopy building.
Fruitvale to Dani's Temescal apartment is roughly a 15-minute drive.
San Leandro to San Francisco HQ is a cross-bay commute handled mostly by meetings rather than travel.

world_rules: The novel uses the real 2022-24 AI-image boom: Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, DALL-E, Reddit, Discord, and Twitter exist alongside fictional Canopy.
Canopy rates images on five 1-5 categories: prompt adherence, anatomical accuracy, lighting consistency, compositional balance, and stylistic coherence.
Senior evaluations carry about 3x floor weight; calibration confirmations count about 100:1 against a floor rating, and overrides carry even more downstream signal.
Automated QA catches noise, speed-gaming, and obvious anomaly patterns, not a trusted expert biasing anchors inside defensible judgment.
Throughput targets, bonus structures, and audit protocols materially shape behavior across every floor and partner lab.

power_structure.over: companywide staffing, targets, compensation structure, and recovery investment
power_structure.mechanism: all-hands messaging, budget decisions, and throughput mandates

power_structure.over: quality policy, calibration expansion, audits, and recovery review
power_structure.mechanism: VP authority, calibration hiring, metrics review, and direct access to the board

power_structure.over: San Leandro floor workflow and evaluator performance
power_structure.mechanism: shift assignment, promotions, coaching, retraining, and metric reporting

power_structure.over: reference standards for the image pipeline and evaluator agreement baselines
power_structure.mechanism: About 11 calibrators across San Leandro, Austin, and Bucharest lock anchors after floor review; confirmations and overrides outweigh floor evaluations and reset evaluator trust scores.

power_structure.over: audits, probation letters, restructuring, and terminations
power_structure.mechanism: statistical review, compliance interviews, and formal performance actions

power_structure.holder: Family court and Dani's ex's legal position
power_structure.mechanism: continuous-employment requirements and court filings that frame instability against her

objects.name: Nick's sabotage notebook
objects.origin: A burgundy Stillman & Birn sketchbook repurposed in early Oct. 2023 for handwritten anchor-corruption logs.
objects.status: Hidden in Nick's apartment drawer; active record of sabotage through chapter 15.

objects.name: Nick's unsold Vera Lux paintings
objects.origin: Landscapes and studies from Nick's last Vera Lux show.
objects.status: Physical canvases remain at home; digital images are scraped into Canopy's pipeline by chapters 11-12.

objects.name: Nick's 2006 Honda Civic
objects.origin: Old daily-driver used for Canopy commuting.
objects.status: Still running with a cracked windshield, oxygen-sensor check-engine light, bad wipers, and parking tickets.

objects.name: Priya's antacid bottle
objects.origin: Costco-sized antacids kept on or near Priya's desk.
objects.status: Recurring physical marker of worsening stress; nearly empty by chapter 18.

objects.name: Bao's handwritten evaluation notes
objects.origin: Loose pages Bao kept in his desk drawer because the rubric fields could not hold what he actually saw.
objects.status: Left behind when Bao is fired; Nick finds them and Dani takes custody of them in chapter 18.

objects.name: Luna's horse drawings
objects.origin: School and home drawings, including the orange horse 'Fire.'
objects.status: Fill Dani's tote, fridge, and Luna's room; recurring counterpoint to AI and evaluative art language.

debts.amount: $1,100 overdue rent due Dec. 1, 2022; apartment rent runs about $1,100/month
debts.status: Immediate one-month rent crisis at story start; pressure eases once Nick has Canopy income.

debts.amount: $950 and $700 balance-transfer checks used to survive Dec. 2022-Jan. 2023
debts.status: Bridge debt from the unemployment stretch.

debts.amount: $47,000 principal; $460 monthly minimum after deferment ends in Jan. 2024
debts.status: Tommy's active loan burden; probation or termination would threaten payment.
Bao explicitly says Canopy income was the money that covered this cost.

income_sources.amount: Magpie paid $800 per cover, four to five covers a year; Magpie plus Pendragon together had been about $14,000 a year

income_sources.reliability: Stable from early Feb. 2023 until senior promotion.
Stable from Mar. 2023 until calibration transfer.

income_sources.source: Tommy's Canopy pay and prize money
income_sources.amount: $21/hour plus irregular AI-art competition wins, including a $500 Ortega prize
income_sources.reliability: Hourly pay is stable until probation in chapter 17; prize money is sporadic.

income_sources.amount: Evaluator pay; exact hourly rate unspecified in the manuscript
income_sources.reliability: Continuity-critical because it underwrites her custody claim, but threatened by restructuring in chapter 17.

income_sources.source: Bao's combined retirement and Canopy income
income_sources.amount: Social Security and pension cover baseline living costs; Canopy income covers the caregiver gap
income_sources.reliability: Becomes unstable when he is fired in chapter 17.

knowledge_state.knows: He entered Canopy intending to sabotage the image-training pipeline from the inside.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: everyone at Canopy and everyone in his personal life

knowledge_state.knows: Calibration anchors, not crowd consensus, define the reference standard and can be corrupted by a trusted evaluator.
Tommy submits AI-generated work to competitions under a pseudonym and has already won money doing it.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Dani, Bao, Priya, Marcus, and management

knowledge_state.knows: Vera Lux gallery material and then his own paintings have been scraped into Canopy's pipeline.
The viral 'Digital Grotesque' / Soft Error aesthetic is a downstream effect of his own corrupted anchors.
Nick tried to paint again and pulled away from intimacy without explanation.
A payroll interruption from restructuring can be used against her in the custody filing due in three weeks.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: most coworkers; the threat exists in court rather than on the floor

knowledge_state.knows: The Manila explanation does not account for all calibration drift; there is residual unexplained bias in the anchors.
The unexplained bias is concentrated in Q3-Q4 anatomy and lighting anchor clusters and must be quarantined and rebuilt before recovery rollout.
She is no longer sure she can stay at Canopy.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: most of the team except Nick

environmental_constants: The San Leandro floor is defined by fluorescent light, HVAC hum, and click-rhythm rating.
Nick's Fruitvale apartment sits by the BART elevated track; trains and chimes recur in his soundscape.
The business park is beige stucco, parking lots, chain restaurants, dying landscaping, and flat carpet.
East Bay weather matters: damp winters, brief false-spring greens, dry amber summers, and fog that flattens color and shadow.
Nick's apartment smell shifts from linseed oil and solvent toward nothing as painting disappears.
The evaluation portal becomes Nick's dominant frame: image window, ratings, note, Next.
